Container.Name: Overhearr
Container.Overview: Overhearr is a self-hosted music request manager for Lidarr, in the spirit of Overseerr. Browse what's hot on ListenBrainz, search MusicBrainz, and one-click an album or whole discography into your Lidarr library. Supports multi-user accounts, encrypted credential storage, and a background reconciliation worker that tracks request status from PENDING through AVAILABLE.
Container.Description:
Overhearr is a self-hosted music request manager for Lidarr.
Features: - Discover trending albums and artists via ListenBrainz + new releases from MusicBrainz (no API keys required) - Search MusicBrainz for any artist or album - One-click request an album or full discography - Background reconciliation tracks each request from PENDING -> PROCESSING -> AVAILABLE - Multi-user with admin/standard roles - Encrypted Lidarr API key storage (AES-256-GCM) - Mobile-friendly responsive UI with light/dark theme
Required: a running Lidarr instance. Discover sources work out of the box — no extra accounts to create.
The container runs internally as uid/gid 1000:1000. Make sure /mnt/user/appdata/overhearr is owned by 1000:1000 before first start.

Config.Name: WebUI port
Config.Description: HTTP port the Overhearr web UI listens on. Change the host side if 5056 is already taken.

Config.Name: Config volume
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the SQLite database and future cache files. The host directory MUST be chowned to 1000:1000 before first start, e.g.: chown -R 1000:1000 /mnt/user/appdata/overhearr

Config.Name: Session secret
Config.Description: 64-char hex string used to sign session cookies. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32

Config.Name: Encryption key
Config.Description: 64-char hex string (32 bytes) used to encrypt the Lidarr API key at rest. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Rotating this AFTER initial setup invalidates stored credentials.

Config.Name: Log level
Config.Description: Pino log verbosity: fatal | error | warn | info | debug | trace

Config.Name: Trust proxy
Config.Description: Set to true if Overhearr is behind nginx, Traefik, SWAG, or Cloudflare. Trusts the immediate upstream hop's X-Forwarded-* headers — required for secure cookies and correct client IPs through a reverse proxy.
